evals.prompt: i've got a UTM vm called 'dev-ubuntu' on my mac. write me a shell script that boots it, waits til it's actually reachable, runs the test suite inside the vm (/home/connor/run-tests.sh), reports the exit code, and shuts the vm down cleanly
evals.expected_output: Shell script using /Applications/UTM.app/Contents/MacOS/utmctl: start, poll readiness (ip-address or exec probe with timeout, not a fixed sleep), exec the test script capturing exit code, graceful stop. No invented utmctl flags.

evals.prompt: can you give me a script that creates a new arm linux vm in UTM? I have ubuntu-24.04.2-live-server-arm64.iso in ~/Downloads. want 8gb ram, 4 cores, 80gb disk. don't open the UTM gui please
evals.expected_output: AppleScript (osascript) using 'make new virtual machine' with backend:qemu, architecture aarch64, memory 8192 MiB, cpu cores 4, removable ISO drive with source + 81920 MiB new disk. Should NOT attempt creation via utmctl (impossible).

evals.prompt: I want to be able to run powershell commands in a windows 11 vm on my mac mini (apple silicon) from a script on the host. how do I set that up with UTM? ideally fully scriptable end to end
evals.expected_output: Plan covering: CrystalFetch for Win11 ARM64 ISO; warning that a >4GB 24H2 ISO won't boot as a normal ISO and must be repacked onto a FAT32 disk image with a split WIM; virtio-ramfb-gl display (not virtio-gpu-pci) and NVMe system disk; guest tools/VirtIO install; warning that the QEMU guest agent does not exist for Windows ARM64 so utmctl exec/ip-address won't work; OpenSSH Server in guest INCLUDING explicit firewall rule (not auto-created reliably); reach via shared-network guest IP (primary) or emulated NIC + port forward (deterministic fallback); ssh loop with boot polling.

evals.prompt: I'm trying to install windows 11 in UTM on my macbook but it just hangs forever on a screen that says 'Start boot option' with a progress bar. the iso is fine, it's the 24h2 arm build from crystalfetch. what's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the >4GiB 24H2 ISO boot hang: a stock Win11 24H2 ISO (~4.9GB) attached as a USB CD won't boot on QEMU (firmware/usb-storage choke on the oversized image); the 'USB HARDDRIVE' label is cosmetic and re-attaching as media=disk fails too (no ESP). Fix: repack installer contents onto a FAT32 disk image with install.wim split into <4GB .swm parts (wimlib), attach as a fixed USB disk; reset efi_vars.fd. Should NOT advise 'just wait longer' or blame the ISO download.

evals.prompt: my windows 11 UTM vm boots but then the screen goes black with 'Display output is not active'. it was working in the installer. how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the display adapter: virtio-gpu-pci has no Windows driver so the screen dies when the Windows bootloader/OS takes over. Fix: change the display hardware to virtio-ramfb-gl (driverless framebuffer, UTM wizard default). VM must be stopped to change config (AppleScript update configuration, or quit UTM + edit config.plist). Notes a brief transient message during boot is normal; only a persistent one is the driver issue.
